title: Cross-border payments corridors, settlement, and integration overview
productContext: Fictional early-stage stablecoin infrastructure provider (product name 'Stablebridge') that helps businesses route cross-border payments through stablecoin rails between a small set of example corridors. The product is pre-launch: corridors, names, and timing shown on this page are illustrative samples for design, not a published rate card or licence. The page is reached from the marketing site's payments section at payments.stablebridge.example.com and targets a business payments audience with a secondary technical-evaluator path. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: business payments manager scoping supported corridors and timing for treasury planning
compliance officer evaluating corridor exposure and operational guardrails before sign-off
treasury lead comparing settlement expectations against existing fiat wire workflows
jobs: understand which corridors Stablebridge supports for design purposes
see illustrative settlement time expectations without being promised guarantees
evaluate the integration approach before deciding whether to request access
check that compliance-relevant guardrails (no licence claims, no guaranteed timing) are respected on the page
requiredJourneys: payments manager opens the page, scans supported corridors, and reaches a corridor detail for a representative pair
treasury lead compares illustrative settlement windows side by side and finds the disclaimers about timing
compliance officer reviews the page and confirms no regulatory or guaranteed-timing claims are made
technical evaluator follows a path from the page into the integration overview
constraints: Do not claim Stablebridge is licensed, authorised, or registered in any market; the company holds no regulatory licences and corridors are illustrative design samples only.
All corridor names, currency pairs, and settlement windows shown are fictional examples for layout purposes and must be labelled as illustrative on the page.
Do not present settlement windows as guarantees; show them as illustrative expectations with an explicit 'not a guarantee' treatment next to every timing value.
Compliance and treasury evaluators must be able to reach corridor and integration detail without exposure to speculative volume, partner-count, or savings claims not supplied in this brief.
Exactly one primary call to action (request access) may lead the page; a secondary link to the integration overview must remain reachable without competing with it.
The product name Stablebridge is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real stablecoin issuer, bank, network, or regulator.

mobileRules: corridor origin and destination selectors stack vertically and remain operable in a single-handed grip
an illustrative-only badge stays attached to each corridor row when the list collapses
timeline rails collapse to a vertical stack without losing the 'not a guarantee' tag
disclaimer text remains readable at the smallest breakpoint without horizontal scrolling
integration steps stack vertically and keep the secondary-action treatment intact
code or schema snippets scroll horizontally inside their own container only
